channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-17
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. In the **scienceverse/metacheck** project, issue #313 was successfully addressed through multiple commits from Daniel Lakens, demonstrating the team's dedication to resolving key issues. Meanwhile, the **giladfeldman/docpluck** project underwent substantial updates, including documentation improvements, code refactoring, and the addition of new features such as optional extraction guardrails and logging. Notably, the **Davidvandijcke/coarse** project made strides in internationalization, adding multilingual support and localized review output, which may have implications for other projects like **JamieCummins/regcheck**, where OSF handling was hardened and support for HTML and OSF was added. Overall, these updates showcase the community's commitment to collaboration, improvement, and innovation.

97 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Most active:

metacheck33 commits
docpluck29 commits
regcheck27 commits
coarse7 commits
auto-zcurve1 commits

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where documentation updates were made, including shipping a handoff and cycle journal for version 2.4.91, as well as adding a root README landing page and syncing normalization and benchmark claims with the current code. The codebase was also refactored to surface fallback exception classes in method metadata and to remove an orphaned duplicate band re-extractor in the 'extract_columns' module. New features were added, including optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout, fallback telemetry counters, and optional logging. Additionally, a continuous integration guard was added to ensure docs-code consistency, and an investigation was queued for items 3 and 8 in the todo list. The rotating pool was also updated to onboard new members, including efendic, maier, and xiao, with rotation now active.

3 commit(s) today:

  • f392957 [main] docs(iterate): v2.4.91 shipped handoff + cycle journal — Gilad Feldman
  • 7edc62f [main] chore(canary): onboard efendic/maier/xiao into rotating pool (rotation now active) — Gilad Feldman
  • 662c4df [main] @ — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 4 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the 'dev', 'feat/221-i18n-localized-review', and 'feat/219-i18n-schema-contract' branches. On the 'dev' branch, OpenRouter Fusion was added as a displayed model option and its dynamic-pricing estimates were fixed, as stated in the commit message "feat: add OpenRouter Fusion as a displayed model option + fix its dynamic-pricing estimates". The 'feat/221-i18n-localized-review' branch saw the addition of localized review output via the --language option, with the commit message noting "feat(i18n): localized review output via --language". The 'dev' and 'feat/219-i18n-schema-contract' branches both had a multilingual schema and API contract added, with the commit message specifying "feat(i18n): multilingual schema + API contract". Additionally, a CodeQL analysis workflow configuration was added to the 'dev' branch.

4 commit(s) today:

  • 6fbfe35 [dev] feat: add OpenRouter Fusion as a displayed model option + fix its dynamic-pricing estimates (#218) — David Van Dijcke
  • 7a0af78 [feat/221-i18n-localized-review] feat(i18n): localized review output via --language (PR-B1) (#221) — Davidvandijcke
  • 64b6761 [dev] feat(i18n): multilingual schema + API contract (PR-A) (#219) (#220) — David Van Dijcke
  • 58378ca [feat/219-i18n-schema-contract] feat(i18n): multilingual schema + API contract (PR-A) (#219) — Davidvandijcke

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 7 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

Work was done on the 'staging' branch, where the reasoning effort for OSS was readded. The OSF handling was hardened, and support for HTML and OSF was also added to the repository. The 'privacy.html' file was updated with new content, and frontend updates were made, including the addition of a light mode. Additionally, bugs related to scrolling and GPT-OSS were fixed, and functionality for Claude was added. The repository also saw fixes for bugs, specifically the scroll and GPT-OSS bugs, which were addressed in a separate commit.

7 commit(s) today:

  • 57b6bc1 [staging] readd reasoning effort for oss — Jamie Cummins
  • 9735187 [staging] harden osf handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8cddeb8 [staging] add html, osf support — Jamie Cummins
  • 978ec81 [staging] Update privacy.html — Jamie Cummins
  • 50f52d9 [staging] frontend updates - light mode! — Jamie Cummins
  • b4dc8e4 [staging] fix scroll and gpt-oss bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 9a99761 [staging] add claude functionality — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →
